description_full:
You are given an array of integers nums, there is a sliding window of size k which is moving from the very left of the array to the very right. You can only see the k numbers in the window. Each time the sliding window moves right by one position.

Return the max sliding window.

 
Example 1:


Input: nums = [1,3,-1,-3,5,3,6,7], k = 3
Output: [3,3,5,5,6,7]
Explanation: 
Window position                Max
---------------               -----
[1  3  -1] -3  5  3  6  7       3
 1 [3  -1  -3] 5  3  6  7       3
 1  3 [-1  -3  5] 3  6  7       5
 1  3  -1 [-3  5  3] 6  7       5
 1  3  -1  -3 [5  3  6] 7       6
 1  3  -1  -3  5 [3  6  7]      7


Example 2:


Input: nums = [1], k = 1
Output: [1]


 
Constraints:


	1 <= nums.length <= 105
	-104 <= nums[i] <= 104
	1 <= k <= nums.length

subtasks.intent: Initialize a monotonic deque with indices of elements from the first window, maintaining decreasing order of values.
Iterate through the rest of the array, updating the deque and recording the maximum for each sliding window.
